frameworks.name: functional columnar organization
frameworks.one_line: primate V1 columns process distinct visual features such as color, orientation, ocular dominance

frameworks.name: optogenetic circuit dissection
frameworks.one_line: channelrhodopsin via lentivirus extended to focal column-scale stimulation in macaques

frameworks.name: domain-based neuroprosthetics
frameworks.one_line: framework motivating linkage of functional networks to targeted perceptual modulation

core_question: Can focal optogenetic stimulation selectively activate function-specific cortical columns and their connected networks in primate visual cortex?
key_assumptions: primate V1 is organized into functionally distinct columns
lentiviral channelrhodopsin delivery yields spatially focal expression
anatomically connected domains share functional preference
manipulations: focal optogenetic stimulation of single functional domain
lentiviral targeting of channelrhodopsins to macaque V1
key_findings: first demonstration of focal channelrhodopsin targeting that stimulates functional domains in macaque
targeted stimulation selectively activates anatomically connected neighboring domains of similar function
interpretations: fine-scale optical stimulation can map domain-based functionally specific networks
approach opens path to domain-based neuroprosthetics linking networks to perception and behavior
